summary: Backend-only testing of the per-broker resilience architecture (token-bucket rate limiting + circuit breakers). All 27 tests passed (100%). Verified: GET /api/rate-limits, GET /api/rate-limits/{broker_id}, POST /api/rate-limits/{broker_id}, POST /api/circuit/{broker_id}/reset, GET /api/audit-logs, GET /api/health. Also confirmed static code checks: rate_limiter.py deleted, no remaining imports from it, broker_manager.py imports from resilience, CircuitOpenError imported in both trading_engine.py and server.py.
critical_code_review_comments: resilience.py _place_single in broker_manager.py correctly imports CircuitOpenError inline (inside method) — works fine but could be moved to module-level import for clarity. Low priority.

reset_circuit() calls _notify_circuit_change which logs an audit entry. The audit entry will show circuit going to 'closed' even though it was a manual admin reset (not organic recovery). Consider adding an explicit AuditEventType for admin resets. Minor.

BrokerResilienceConfig.burst field is stored in MongoDB config and returned in get_status(), but is not currently used by the AsyncLimiter constructor (only max_rps is passed to AsyncLimiter). burst param is ignored in _create_limiter. This is a silent mismatch between the stored config and actual limiter behaviour.
seed_data_creation: POST /api/rate-limits/alpaca_test used during tests — synthetic broker 'alpaca_test' config stored in MongoDB. This is non-destructive and can be ignored or cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Resilience architecture fully tested and working. rate_limiter.py is gone. All endpoints respond correctly. The only notable code observation (not a bug) is that the 'burst' field in BrokerResilienceConfig is stored and returned in the config response but is NOT actually passed to AsyncLimiter (only max_rps is used). The limiter is created as AsyncLimiter(cfg.max_rps, 1.0) — the burst parameter has no effect on actual rate limiting. This may be intentional (burst tracking only) or an oversight worth addressing.
